zh.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag於大約1220年出生在瑞典。他和Sigrid Natt och Dag至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag于大约1220年出生在瑞典。他和Sigrid Natt och Dag至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Mattsson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Matts, Nils, Bjorn, Gunvor, Per, Sten, Thor, Thorvald, Torvald.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Matts, a short form of Matthias or Mattheus (see Matthew ). Compare Matson and Mattson .

variants.name: Mattson
meanings: Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Mattsson  .
Americanized form of Danish and Norwegian   Mattsen   and   Madsen  , cognates of 1.
English (mainly Yorkshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Matson  .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Natt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Anit, Gagan, Jagbir, Malkit, Nasib.
Irish or Scottish: shortened form of   McNatt  .
Jewish (American): shortened and altered form of   Nathan  .

variants.name: McNatt
meanings: Irish and Scottish: variant of   McNutt  .

nameMeanings.name: och
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: southern and Silesian variant of   Ach  .
Flemish (Van Och): habitational name for someone from Ogbrugga in Mark (East Flanders), or Ogmolen or Ogpoort in Sint-Renelde (Brabant).
Amerindian (Mexico): Mayan name, from ooch ‘opossum’.

variants.name: Ach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Manfred, Ilse.
German: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, from Old High German aha ‘running water’.
German: habitational name from any of the places so named in southern Germany and Austria.

nameMeanings.name: Dag
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Dachs  .
French: habitational name, with fused preposition d(e), for someone from Ax-les-Thermes in Ariège.
English: variant of Dack , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Dachs
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zalman, Dovid, Heshy, Shimon, Simcha.  German Alois.
German: from Middle High German dahs ‘badger’, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a badger or an occupational name for a badger hunter.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Dachs ‘badger’.

nameMeanings.name: Bo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Danish: originally a byname for a householder, derived from Old Norse búa ‘to dwell, have a household’.

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

en.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag was born about 1220, in Sweden. He had at least 1 son with Sigrid Natt och Dag.

fr.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag est né environ 1220, à Suède. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sigrid Natt och Dag.

de.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag wurde um 1220 in Schweden geboren. Er hatte mit Sigrid Natt och Dag mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag è nato il circa 1220 nel Svezia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sigrid Natt och Dag.

ja.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dagは約1220年にスウェーデンで生まれた。彼はSigrid Natt och Dagとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag(은)는 대략 1220년 스웨덴에서 태어났다. 그는 Sigrid Natt och Dag(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag nasceu aproximadamente 1220, em Suécia. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sigrid Natt och Dag.

ru.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag родился около 1220 г. в местности Швеция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sigrid Natt och Dag.

es.lifeSummary: Bo Mattsson Natt och Dag nació aproximadamente 1220, en Suecia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sigrid Natt och Dag.
